hero.title: Operations for condominiums and residential towers—one platform.
hero.lead: BuildingFex helps boards and property managers run common areas, collections, maintenance, and communications with the same experience on this site and in the web application.

cards.title: Common areas
cards.text: Bookings, rules, and upkeep in one view.
cards.detail: Configure calendars, capacity limits, and approval flows so residents book pools, gyms, and shared rooms without overloading staff. Post rules, attach checklists, and keep maintenance tied to each space so nothing slips between spreadsheets and chat threads.

cards.title: Collections
cards.text: Fees, reminders, and payment visibility for owners.
cards.detail: Define fee schedules, issue reminders, and give owners a clear picture of balances and history. Finance teams can track partial payments, notes, and exceptions in one place—reducing disputes and repeated back-and-forth over email.

cards.title: Maintenance
cards.text: Requests, vendors, and status for the whole building.
cards.detail: Open work orders from residents or staff, assign vendors, and follow status from reported to closed. Attach photos, set priorities, and keep a timeline the board can audit—so recurring issues and SLAs stay visible instead of buried in messages.

cards.title: Announcements
cards.text: Official notices to residents and committees.
cards.detail: Publish building-wide or targeted notices with attachments and clear effective dates. Residents see what matters in context, and committees retain a traceable record of official communications instead of scattered PDFs in inboxes.

cards.title: Access & visitors
cards.detail: Pre-register guests, log vehicles, and align front desk or guard workflows with your access policy. A consistent register helps security and the board answer “who was on site?” without digging through paper logs or ad hoc spreadsheets.

cards.title: Documents
cards.text: Minutes, regulations, and files for the community.
cards.detail: Centralize minutes, regulations, insurance, and contracts with sensible permissions for board, managers, and residents where appropriate. Versioning and search mean fewer “send me that file again” requests and faster onboarding for new administrators.

subscriptions.heading: Subscriptions
subscriptions.ctaDisabledHint: Set VITE_WEB_APP_URL in your environment to enable this link.

plans.name: Essential
features: Built for smaller buildings and boutique condominiums.
Common areas, collections, and announcements in one place.
Documents and visitor logs for your community.
Web console for administrators and boards.

plans.name: Standard
plans.highlight: Billed monthly in PEN. Covers up to 40 units.
features: Designed for mid-size towers and active communities.
Same capabilities as Essential with higher capacity.
Clearer visibility into fees and delinquency.
Broadcast updates to residents and committees.

plans.name: Scale
plans.highlight: Billed monthly in PEN. Tier set to avoid overlap with 16–40.
features: Performance and limits matched to building size.
Reporting for larger boards and committees.
Help rolling out and onboarding your team.
Support channel with agreed response times.

features.heading: Why property managers choose BuildingFex
features.headingOrange:
a strategic ally
for your building
paragraphs: We bring digital tools into everyday operations—from finances to resident communications—so boards and managers work with clarity and fewer manual steps.
Our roadmap pairs solid engineering with transparent terms and professional ethics, so communities can trust both the platform and the process.
Whether you oversee a small building or a large tower, you get a consistent experience on this site and inside the web application.

items.title: Technology as a strategic ally
items.text: We integrate digital tools across processes—from financial management to resident communication—so nothing important lives in disconnected spreadsheets.

items.title: Technology with BI (Business Intelligence)
items.text: We use business intelligence to produce reports with clear indicators for efficient, data-informed decisions.

items.title: AI across internal workflows
items.text: We apply artificial intelligence to automate tasks, optimize resources, and support better decision-making.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.lead: Three steps to move your building into one digital home.

steps.title: Register your building
steps.text: Set units, roles, and contact channels so boards, managers, and residents stay aligned.

steps.title: Configure workflows
steps.text: Define common areas, fees, announcements, and access policies that match your community.

steps.title: Operate with visibility
steps.text: Track collections, maintenance, and reports without scattered spreadsheets.

testimonials.heading: What customers say
testimonials.lead: Managers and boards that value clarity and fewer manual handoffs.

items.quote: We moved from email threads and spreadsheets to one dashboard. Delinquency dropped because everyone sees the same numbers.
items.name: María Castillo

items.quote: Residents understand notices better, and common-area bookings stopped colliding on the calendar.
items.name: Luis Ortega

items.quote: Support was quick during rollout and the team adopted the web app without friction.
items.name: Ana Ríos

faqItems.question: What is BuildingFex?
faqItems.answer: A platform to run condominiums and residential buildings—common areas, collections, maintenance, and resident communications—with the same experience on this site and in the web app.

faqItems.question: How do I pick a subscription plan?
faqItems.answer: Choose based on how many apartments your building has. Amounts are in Peruvian soles (PEN) and billing is monthly. If you’re unsure at a range boundary, contact us via Support.

faqItems.question: How do I contact the team?
faqItems.answer: Email hola@ejemplo.com or open a ticket from Support in your profile once you have access to the application.

faqItems.question: Is my data protected?
faqItems.answer: We follow solid security practices and clear terms. Legal details are in the terms of service linked in the footer.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.lead: This page is a placeholder. Replace with your privacy policy before production.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.updated: Last updated: April 2026. Placeholder document for coursework; replace with counsel-reviewed text before production.

sections.title: 1. Purpose and ethics
sections.body: BuildingFex provides digital services under principles aligned with the ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct and the IEEE Code of Ethics: honesty, fairness, privacy, and competence. We commit to disclose limitations of our software and to prioritize user safety and data stewardship.

sections.title: 2. Acceptable use
sections.body: You agree not to misuse the platform—including attempting unauthorized access, interfering with other users, or using the service to violate applicable law. Educational institutions remain responsible for compliance with local regulations (FERPA, GDPR analogues, etc.) where they apply.

sections.title: 3. Accounts and access
sections.body: Access to the web application may require credentials issued by BuildingFex or your institution. You are responsible for safeguarding credentials and for activity under your account.

sections.title: 4. Intellectual property
sections.body: The BuildingFex name, visual identity, and software are protected. Unless expressly permitted, you may not copy, modify, or redistribute our materials outside the scope of your license or institutional agreement.

sections.title: 5. Disclaimers
sections.body: Services are provided “as is” to the extent permitted by law. BuildingFex does not guarantee uninterrupted availability. Nothing in these terms constitutes legal or professional advice; institutions should obtain independent counsel for contractual matters.

sections.title: 6. Contact
sections.body: For questions about these terms, contact the BuildingFex team using the email provided in the contact section of this landing page.
